formulas.name: Data Link Layer Frame Format
formulas.formula: Preamble + Header + Data + FCS (Frame Check Sequence)
formulas.variables: Preamble: Synchronization bytes, Header: Source and destination MAC addresses, Data: Data being transmitted, FCS: Error detection
formulas.notes: Used to understand the structure of data link layer frames

formulas.name: OSI Reference Model
formulas.formula: Physical Layer + Data Link Layer + Network Layer + Transport Layer + Session Layer + Presentation Layer + Application Layer
formulas.notes: A conceptual framework used to understand how data is transmitted over a network

formulas.name: TCP/IP Model
formulas.formula: Network Access Layer + Internet Layer + Transport Layer + Session Layer + Presentation Layer + Application Layer
formulas.notes: Simpler version of OSI model, widely used today

formulas.name: Slotted Aloha Protocol Throughput
formulas.variables: Throughput: Network throughput, Slot duration: Time taken to transmit a slot, Packet duration: Time taken to transmit a packet
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the throughput of a slotted Aloha network

formulas.name: Polling Protocol Throughput
formulas.formula: Throughput = (1 / Number of Devices) * (1 / (Transmission Time per Device))
formulas.variables: Throughput: Network throughput, Number of Devices: Number of devices in the network, Transmission Time per Device: Time taken for a device to transmit
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the throughput of a polling network

formulas.name: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection (CSMA/CD) Throughput
formulas.variables: Throughput: Network throughput, Bit rate: Data transmission rate, Collision time: Time taken to detect and resolve a collision, Packet size: Size of the data packet
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the throughput of a CSMA/CD network

formulas.name: Network Topology
formulas.formula: Bus Topology: Linear, Star Topology: Central node, Mesh Topology: Fully connected, Ring Topology: Circular
formulas.variables: Network Topology: Types of network topologies
formulas.notes: Used to understand the different types of network topologies
